preamble.p: This Constitution establishes the Organizational Architecture of the TerraModulus Project as the foundational framework governing the superior Realm for the entire Project, administrating the Community, supervising the Projects and Policies, moderating the Rules and Regulations, and executing the Elements and Procedures for the entire Organizational Architecture.

content.p: On the whole, Governing Executive Systems maintain their own Roles and Purposes without overlaps; and one may hierarchize the other; specific definitions shall be made in corresponding Charters.
Executive Directives (EDs) shall be initiated by a Governing Board for provisional or possibly permanent executions for the Governing System.

Community Standards and Guidelines (CSG) include selections from EPCs for public reference to applicable Rules and Regulations for the Community; Execution Standards Compilation (ESC) includes selections from EFPs for public reference to available Standards and Specifications for the Projects.
This Constitution and underlying Governing Systems shall uphold the supreme authority.
Jurisdiction of this Constitution shall always be covered under international regulations. All acts in local areas shall also be covered under respective local jurisdictions.
Members of the Bodies hold Roles in Governance of this Constitution, with certain obligations and liabilities depending on their Powers and Permissions.
The Bodies hold authority over this Constitution.
The Host Organization of this Constitution holds supreme authority via a Governing Board.
A Governing Board oversees and guides the Governing Council for administration and operation.
An Executive Leadership holds authority over the Executives. The Executive Leadership shall be decided by the Host Organization of this Constitution without participation of Community.
An optional Chief Governance Officer may be appointed to cooperate with the Leadership in ensuring compliance with this Constitution and underlying Proposals and Charters.
A Governing Council discusses and votes Proposals being drafted or passed.
The Council oversee the Community by Process of Proposals.
A Governing Executive Committee supervises the Governing Council.
Members of the Committee shall be elected and appointed from the Council.
The Committee may also discuss Proposals during discussions in the Council.
The Committee votes passed Proposals by the Council for Ratification, which accords to the results of voting in the Council.
Most governing acts by the Governing Bodies are under the Directorial Branch.
Proposals are the primary Executive operations in Governance, objectively driven by the Community.

In the Executive and Governing levels, Proposals shall outline how a project, procedure or initiative will be executed, managed, and monitored to achieve its objectives, or, explain or define a certain mechanism which would be applied onto this Constitution, or, behave as a governing procedure for other purposes of Proposals.
Any Participant or Member of this Constitution maintain the Rights to initiate Proposals, which shall potentially make changes to or affect any Part of the entire Architecture.
A Proposal may potentially represent stances of the Governing Bodies or the overall Project by any means regardless.
A Proposal to initiations to a Research Dossier shall be proposed via this Process; the Proposal shall optionally appoint Members of the Community for the Research Dossier.
Whenever a Proposal being Standards and Specifications for the Project, it shall be submitted to Execution Standards Compilation (ESC).
Charters may be iterated by versions in different Streams of Charters.
Whenever a Charter being Policies, Rules and Regulations for the Community, it shall be submitted to Community Standards and Guidelines (CSG).
All Participants and Members shall adhere to the Charters whenever their designated administrative scope and obligations are applicable.
When an Executive Directive (ED) is proposed by the Governing Board, it shall be self-ratified.
Whenever a Directive affects this Constitution, a Proposal or a Charter, a definite time limit must be declared; otherwise, the limit may be omitted.
In most cases, a Directive shall not affect this Constitution.
The Managerial Branch is responsible for administrative operations in this Constitution.
Community Moderators may be elected from the Community and appointed by the Governing Bodies to aid in moderation of the Community.
Appointed members and voluntary participants shall conduct researches and investigations as per a corresponding ratified Executive Research Dossier (ERD).

Executive Research Dossiers (ERDs) are structured summaries or compilations of research findings, insights, and recommendations to provide high-level overviews of research foundations, and shall provide suggestions and advice to governing operations.
Research Dossiers are filed by Topics of Researches, sequential by stages or times in Streams.
All Members of the Community have the rights to supervise the Governing Bodies through participation and discussion in administrative activities.
In cases of dereliction of duty by the Governing Bodies, the Community may challenge individuals through corresponding procedures defined in Charters.
This Constitution shall be amended only by extraordinary consensus for Ratification from the Supreme Governing Group.
The Governing Council, whenever Members shall deem it necessary, shall propose an iterated Version of this Constitution with all Amendments to be made in a Version.
All governing resources such as iterations of this Constitution and the Executive Systems, must preserve historical versions for transparency. All amendments and archives must be publicly documented.
